summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the BZA and Zoning Commission to discuss potential text amendments to the Township Zoning Resolution. Key topics included temporary dwelling regulations for new construction and major renovations, shipping container regulations, tiny homes, and a proposal to increase the minimum lot size in R1 districts to two acres to help preserve rural character and slow development.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:21 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Liz Field to finalize temporary dwelling text amendment language by February 27 trustee meeting
Liz Field to tweak the major renovation definition to be clearer and potentially include hardship language
Liz Field to research existing code provisions for temporary RV parking/use by visitors on residential properties
Liz Field to research shipping container regulations, including contacting Lucas County/Springfield Township planning director
Liz Field to look into accessory dwelling unit regulations and potential expansion for mother-in-law suite type structures
Staff to prepare land use plan maps showing R1 parcels of two acres or more for next workshop
Administrator Rose to collect cell phone numbers from BZA and Zoning Commission members for text notifications
Staff to send out survey to schedule a March zoning workshop
Lisa to handle the February 27 trustee meeting presentation of temporary dwelling language in Liz's absence
key_amounts: Average tiny home size: 225 square feet
Tiny homes generally under 600 square feet
Average tiny home cost: just over $50,000
Current minimum dwelling size: 1,400 square feet
Current accessory dwelling unit minimum lot area: 10,000 square feet
Toledo tiny home lots: 40 by 80 feet (3,200 square feet)
notable_topics: Temporary dwelling regulations for new construction and major renovations (RV/camper use during building)
Shipping container regulations for residential and commercial use
Tiny homes - both as primary dwellings and accessory dwelling units
Mother-in-law suite / accessory dwelling unit regulations
Proposal to increase R1 minimum lot size to two acres to preserve rural character
RV parking regulations for visitors on residential properties
Need for industrial zoning to offset residential tax burden
Land use plan review and preservation of agricultural land
Concern about rapid development similar to Lebanon, Mason, and West Chester
notes: This was a joint workshop between the Board of Trustees, BZA, and Zoning Commission with no public forum. The temporary dwelling text amendment has been discussed approximately six times over two years since the tornado but was never officially adopted. Clermont County commissioners are building a veterans village with tiny homes in the Bethel area, which was referenced as a precedent. Trustee Allen referenced a conversation with Lucas County/Springfield Township planning director at the Ohio Township Association Winter Conference regarding shipping containers. There was significant discussion about preserving Goshen's rural character against development pressure, with multiple participants sharing personal connections to the community.
title: Special BOT Zoning Workshop 2-12-24
